title: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan — ₹3,000 Pension for Unorganized Workers
meta_description: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan 2026 — ₹3,000/month pension after 60 for unorganized workers. Contribution ₹55-200, government matches equally. How to apply.

hero.one_liner: Guaranteed ₹3,000/month pension after age 60 for unorganized sector workers — government contributes equally to your monthly deposit

what_is_it: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM) is a voluntary pension scheme for unorganized sector workers launched on 15 February 2019. It guarantees a monthly pension of ₹3,000 after the worker turns 60. The unique feature is that the government matches your contribution — for every rupee you put in, the government puts an equal amount. As of March 2026, over 52.5 lakh workers have enrolled.

The monthly contribution varies by age at enrollment: ₹55/month if you join at 18 (just ₹1.8/day), increasing to ₹200/month if you join at 40. These are very small amounts — even a daily wage worker earning ₹300-500/day can comfortably contribute. The government's equal matching effectively doubles your investment.

The scheme targets workers like street vendors, domestic helpers, auto/rickshaw drivers, construction workers, rag pickers, agricultural laborers, and all other workers in the unorganized sector with monthly income up to ₹15,000. Workers should not be members of EPF, NPS (government), or ESIC. This covers over 42 crore unorganized workers in India.

After the subscriber turns 60, they receive ₹3,000/month pension for life. If the subscriber dies, the spouse receives 50% pension (₹1,500/month) for their lifetime. If both pass away, the accumulated corpus is returned to the nominee. The scheme provides social security to the most vulnerable workforce.

eligibility.label: Worker type
eligibility.value: Unorganized sector worker — NOT member of EPF/NPS(Govt)/ESIC

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card, savings bank account/Jan Dhan account

eligibility.label: Enrollment
eligibility.value: Visit any CSC (Common Service Centre) — enrollment cannot be done online by self

extra_sections.heading: Complete Contribution & Pension Table by Age
Why PM-SYM Beats Traditional Alternatives
rows: Yes, but you need formal employment

extra_sections.heading: How PM-SYM Actually Works — Real Example
content: Let's say you're a 25-year-old rickshaw driver earning ₹350/day. You join PM-SYM: Your contribution = ₹100/month.
Over 35 years (age 25 to 60), total contributions = ₹84,000. But the pension fund is invested and grows.
At age 60, you receive ₹3,000/month = ₹36,000/year. If you live 20 years post-60 (age 80), total pension received = ₹7,20,000.
That's 8.5× your total contributions. The government match, compound interest, and pension fund returns make this possible.
Another example: A 40-year-old domestic helper joins PM-SYM for only 20 years (age 40 to 60). She contributes ₹200/month × 12 months × 20 years = ₹48,000 total.
Government matches with ₹48,000. Total corpus = ₹96,000 + interest.
She still gets ₹3,000/month pension for life. This shows the scheme works even for late joiners.
If a beneficiary dies before age 60: All contributions (beneficiary + government) are returned to the nominee. No loss, no forfeiture.

steps.title: Visit nearest CSC center
steps.description: Find CSC at locator.csccloud.in. Carry Aadhaar card, savings bank passbook (with Aadhaar linked), and mobile number. You cannot self-register online — enrollment must be done at CSC.

steps.title: CSC operator enrolls you on maandhan.in
steps.description: The operator enters your details, verifies Aadhaar via OTP/biometric, captures your photo, and registers you on the PM-SYM portal. Choose auto-debit from your bank account.

steps.title: Monthly auto-debit starts
steps.description: Your contribution is auto-debited from your savings/Jan Dhan account every month. Ensure sufficient balance. Missed payments can be paid later without penalty during the same financial year.

steps.title: Pension starts at 60
steps.description: On your 60th birthday, ₹3,000/month pension begins. Credited directly to your bank account. Continues for lifetime. No paperwork needed annually.

how_to_apply.title: Visit any CSC center with Aadhaar + bank passbook
how_to_apply.description: Find nearest CSC at locator.csccloud.in or ask at your Gram Panchayat office.

how_to_apply.title: Enroll through the CSC operator
how_to_apply.description: Operator verifies identity and registers you on maandhan.in portal.

how_to_apply.title: Set up monthly auto-debit
how_to_apply.description: ₹55-200/month debited from your bank account. Government matches equally.

how_to_apply.title: Receive pension from age 60
how_to_apply.description: ₹3,000/month for life. Spouse gets ₹1,500/month after subscriber's death.

important_alert.text: You can choose EITHER PM-SYM or Atal Pension Yojana — not both. If you want a pension higher than ₹3,000/month, choose APY (up to ₹5,000/month). If you want government matching contribution, choose PM-SYM. Once enrolled, you cannot switch.

important_dates.label: Enrollment
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at CSC centers

faqs.question: Can I exit PM-SYM before 60?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you only get back your own contributions with bank savings interest rate — you lose the government's matching contribution and all investment returns. Exit is allowed anytime but is not recommended unless absolutely necessary. If you exit after contributing for 10 years, you lose ₹50,000+ in government match.

faqs.question: I'm already in APY. Can I join PM-SYM?
faqs.answer: No. PM-SYM and APY are mutually exclusive — you can be in only one. If you're already contributing to APY, you cannot join PM-SYM and vice versa. Choose based on your needs: APY for flexibility (₹1-5K pension), PM-SYM for government matching (fixed ₹3K).

faqs.question: What if I miss monthly contributions?
faqs.answer: If you miss a monthly payment, you can pay the pending amount later during the same financial year without any penalty. If contributions remain unpaid beyond 2 years, the account may be deactivated. Contact your CSC to regularize pending payments. Missing contributions doesn't affect your pension eligibility once you catch up.

faqs.question: I earn more than ₹15,000/month now. Will my enrollment be cancelled?
faqs.answer: Income is self-declared at the time of enrollment and is not continuously verified. If your income increases after enrollment, your existing enrollment continues. However, if you join EPFO/ESIC through formal employment, you should technically exit PM-SYM as you'd now be in the organized sector.

faqs.question: Is the ₹3,000 pension taxable?
faqs.answer: Technically yes — pension is treated as income under income tax law. But for most PM-SYM beneficiaries, ₹3,000/month (₹36,000/year) is well below the basic tax exemption limit (₹2.5+ lakh for seniors), so effectively no tax is payable in practice.

faqs.question: What if I die before turning 60?
faqs.answer: Your entire accumulated corpus (your contributions + government matching + interest) is paid to your nominee as a lump sum. You don't lose the government match — your family gets the full amount. This is different from some other pension schemes where death before maturity means forfeiture.

faqs.question: Can I use PM-SYM while working in my current job?
faqs.answer: Yes. PM-SYM doesn't conflict with your job. If you're self-employed, you can contribute. If you're formally employed under EPF/ESIC, you should not join. If you work multiple informal jobs (e.g., daily wage worker + part-time vendor), you can definitely join PM-SYM.
